finding_results.notes: Beat 1 (Tommy's table challenge, line 83-87): Monologue broken into two shorter pressured turns with Joel's interrupted protest between them. Tommy now delivers short coaching-mode declaratives instead of a balanced mini-speech. Beat 2 (Tommy's moral verdict, line 285): Stripped composed framing ('Well, I'm saying it plain' opener, 'Full stop' device, 'I still loved the guy yesterday morning' temporal specificity). Now three blunt short sentences with colloquial 'hid' instead of formal 'hidden'. Beat 3 (Paul's lake-facing diagnosis, lines 319/323): Replaced double-parallel composed closers ('I'm not calling it private. I'm not helping Martin keep it tidy from the grave.') with single direct closer ('I'm not covering for him.'). Paul's speech tightened to stress register — qualifiers dropped, sentences shortened, no rhetorical parallelism. Beat 4 (Joel's final line, line 339): Already revised in prior pass from the snapshot's portable thematic quote ('a dead friend who was better than this and also exactly this') to a practical, specific desire ('I want it to stop sounding like I picked a fight for no reason'). No further change needed.

summary: Roughened four beat-closing speeches flagged as too composed. Three of four beats revised: Tommy's table challenge broken into shorter turns with Joel's interrupted protest; Tommy's moral verdict stripped to blunt coaching cadence with colloquial texture; Paul's diagnosis compressed from parallel composed closers to a single direct stress-register closer. Joel's final line already passed the acceptance test from a prior revision. All tracked payoffs (joel_argument reveal), secondary character beats (Tommy's hollow certainty, Doug's quiet recognition), and chapter-ending state preserved. No new continuity issues introduced.
